commit.message:
fix: use Python for SSH key parsing to handle literal \n in secret

The SSH_PRIVATE_KEY secret stores newlines as literal \n characters.
Bash sed/echo have escaping issues in YAML. Python handles this reliably.
Also added key validation with clear error message on failure.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle SSH key stored as single line with literal \n

The SSH_PRIVATE_KEY secret has newlines stored as literal \n characters,
resulting in a 1-line key file. Added sed to convert them back.

commit.message:
@wordpress/ui: add Collapsible component (#76280)

* Add Collapsible component to @wordpress/ui

Thin wrapper around Base UI's Collapsible component with Root,
Trigger, and Panel subcomponents. No custom styles — this is a
pure pass-through of the Base UI API.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add Storybook stories for Collapsible

Covers default, default-open, disabled, and controlled variants.

Made-with: Cursor

* Add unit tests for Collapsible

Covers ref forwarding, uncontrolled/controlled open state,
disabled behavior, and className pass-through.

Made-with: Cursor

* Update CHANGELOG for Collapsible component

Made-with: Cursor

* Address review feedback

- Fix import ordering: external (@base-ui) before @wordpress
- Add render prop tests for Root, Trigger, and Panel

Made-with: Cursor

* Update CollapsibleCard to use internal Collapsible wrapper

Replace direct @base-ui/react/collapsible imports with the new
internal Collapsible component in all three CollapsibleCard files.

In header.tsx, derive the open state from the aria-expanded prop
instead of the Base UI state callback, since our wrapper's render
prop type uses a single-argument signature.

Made-with: Cursor

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add comment explaining aria-expanded tradeoff in header render prop

The Collapsible wrapper's ComponentProps utility narrows the render
callback to a single-argument signature, so Base UI's `state` object
isn't available. Document why we read open state from `aria-expanded`.

Made-with: Cursor

* avoid unnecessary destructuring

* add hidden until found example

---------

Co-authored-by: ciampo <mciampini@git.wordpress.org>
Co-authored-by: mirka <0mirka00@git.wordpress.org>

commit.message:
Fix PY260 JPEG output via SCCB reset workaround

The pre-compiled mega_ccm driver's set_pixformat() doesn't properly reset
the PY260 sensor, leaving it stuck in raw YUV422 mode. This commit adds:

- Direct SCCB register writes to reset PY260 with correct timing (100ms
  hold + 1500ms settle) and configure JPEG mode, resolution, and quality
- Patched cam_hal.c with 512KB JPEG receive buffer (default formula was
  too small for PY260 output) and diagnostic counters for debugging
- Private esp32-camera headers (cam_hal.h, ll_cam.h) required by the
  cam_hal.c override
- Production settings: VGA resolution, high quality, double-buffered
- Updated README with technical documentation

Tested: /capture returns 27KB JPEG, /stream delivers MJPEG, /status
returns JSON. All endpoints verified working on hardware.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

v2.0.0 — First production release (#74)

* docs: add UX/UI feedback plan and serve:php dev script

* Release v1.4.1 with new features and improvements

Updated changelog to reflect version 1.4.1 release with added features.

* Initial plan

* Implement UX/UI improvements: sticky CTA, clickable cards, form single-column, URL pre-select, hero headlines, nav label, whitespace, disable captcha debug

Co-authored-by: halukmt <161864399+halukmt@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix CSS specificity: use ID selector for whitespace overrides instead of class-only selectors

Co-authored-by: halukmt <161864399+halukmt@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix nav home: restore data-nav-label to preserve house icon alongside visible Start text

Co-authored-by: halukmt <161864399+halukmt@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* feat: implement multi-language SEO for all 9 languages

- Add multi-language URL routing to .htaccess (en, pl, hu, sk, cs, it, bg, ro prefixes)
- Update lang.js with URL-based language detection and dynamic canonical/og:locale updates
- Add complete hreflang tags (9 languages + x-default) to all 9 main HTML pages
- Add all 8 og:locale:alternate tags to all 9 main HTML pages
- Update sitemap.xml from 8 German-only URLs to 72 URLs (8 pages × 9 languages)"

Co-authored-by: halukmt <161864399+halukmt@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: language switcher navigates to language-prefixed URL; fix nav links and card hrefs

- Language switcher now navigates to /{lang}/{page} instead of just updating translations in place
- adjustNavLinks() now includes the current language prefix in all nav link hrefs
- Apartment card buttons now use /wohnung?id=... (pretty URL) on production
- .htaccess: add [QSA] to wohnung-detail canonical redirect to preserve ?id= query string"

Co-authored-by: halukmt <161864399+halukmt@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use absolute resource paths; language switcher skips URL nav on localhost

- All component/asset fetches now use absolute paths (/components/, /lang/, /assets/)
  so they work correctly from any URL depth including /en/wohnungen, /pl/, etc.
- Language switcher only navigates to /{lang}/page on production;
  on localhost http-server has no .htaccess support so setLanguage() is used in-place
- Removed now-unused IS_PAGES and inPages variables from main.js
- Same absolute-path fix applied to lang.js (injectHtmlPartial, translation loader)
- Same fix applied to whatsapp-fab.js

Co-authored-by: halukmt <161864399+halukmt@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add router.php for php -S with URL routing and custom 404 page

- router.php mirrors all .htaccess rewrite rules for local php -S dev server
- Language-prefixed URLs (/en/wohnungen, /pl/kontakt etc.) now work on localhost
- Custom 404 page (pages/404.html) served for unmatched URLs instead of PHP's purple default
- README updated: recommend `php -S localhost:8080 router.php` over `php -S localhost:8080 -t .`

Co-authored-by: halukmt <161864399+halukmt@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add npm run dev script; emphasize router.php requirement in README

- Add `npm run dev` shortcut (php -S localhost:8080 router.php) to package.json
- README: add warning callout that router.php is required for custom 404 and language URLs
- Confirmed router.php correctly serves custom 404.html (HTTP 404) for all unknown URLs

Co-authored-by: halukmt <161864399+halukmt@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add Docker dev environment with Apache + Mailpit (closes #70)

- Add .docker/Dockerfile (php:8.2-apache, mod_rewrite/headers/expires, GD, msmtp)
- Add .docker/apache.conf (AllowOverride All so .htaccess is fully active)
- Add .docker/php.ini (sendmail via msmtp, display_errors On)
- Add .docker/msmtprc (relay to mailpit:1025, gitignored)
- Add docker-compose.yml (web on :8080, mailpit UI on :8025)
- Fix session cookie domain in api/sendmail.php, csrf.php, captcha.php:
  hardcoded .derko-immobilien.de replaced with DERKO_COOKIE_DOMAIN env var
  (Docker sets empty string so sessions work on localhost)
- Replace serve:php script with docker:up / docker:down in package.json
- Add .docker/msmtprc to .gitignore

* fix: resolve Docker port conflicts on Windows/Hyper-V

Ports 7981-8080 are reserved by Hyper-V on this machine.
- Web server: 8080 → 8081
- Mailpit UI: 8025 → 9000
- Fix GD extension build: install libpng/libjpeg/libfreetype6 before docker-php-ext-install gd

* docs: update README with Docker dev mode and startup options

* chore: remove unused dummy.txt

* fix: active nav highlight for pretty URLs

markActive() normalized slug to .html before map lookup so /ueber-uns
and similar pretty URL paths are correctly highlighted in the nav.

* docs: add v1.4.2 changelog entry for Docker dev setup

* Initial plan

* feat: add SEO clean URLs and sitemap for apartment detail pages

- .htaccess: add rewrite rules for /wohnung/<slug> and /<lang>/wohnung/<slug>, 301 redirect old ?id= URLs
- router.php: add slug URL routing with 301 redirect for old ?id= format
- wohnung-detail.js: extract key from pathname slug, update canonical/hreflang to use clean URLs
- main.js: update card links to clean slug URLs, fix nav highlighting for slug URLs
- sitemap.xml: add 63 apartment entries (7 apartments × 9 languages)
- lang/*/*.json: update all 90 FAQ links to clean slug URLs

Co-authored-by: halukmt <161864399+halukmt@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: add Playwright E2E test suite (282 tests) and bug fixes — v1.5.0

- Playwright E2E test suite with 282 tests across 4 spec files (routing,
  canonical-seo, contact-form, pages) covering all 9 languages, URL routing,
  CSRF/CAPTCHA/rate-limit security, and apartment detail pages
- api/test-helper.php: debug-only endpoint for CAPTCHA code access in tests
- npm scripts: test, test:ui, test:report
- Fix: removed data-i18n from <title> in wohnung-detail.html (applyTranslations
  was overwriting the dynamic apartment title set by wohnung-detail.js)
- Fix: Apache directory listing disabled in .docker/apache.conf (Options
  FollowSymLinks instead of Indexes FollowSymLinks)
- Fix: mail() return value checked in sendmail.php with error logging
- Fix: absolute canonical URLs in wohnung-detail.js
- docs: copilot-instructions.md updated with full Playwright documentation
- chore: playwright-report/ and playwright-results/ added to .gitignore

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: language-aware URL navigation across all pages — v1.5.1

Root causes fixed:
- All pages/HTML files used relative CSS paths (../assets/css/style.css)
  which resolved to 404 on language-prefixed URLs (/en/, /pl/, etc.)
  → Changed all paths to absolute (/assets/css/style.css)
- index.html used relative JS paths (assets/js/lang.js) causing blank
  page on /en/ and other lang-prefixed root URLs
  → Changed all JS paths to absolute
- adjustNavLinks() used isLocal check instead of pretty URL detection,
  causing nav and footer links to lose language prefix when navigating
  → Replaced isLocal with usePrettyUrls = !pathname.includes('.html')
  → Extended to update ALL internal links (not just nav IDs)
- Language switcher did not persist language to localStorage before
  navigating, so switching back to German showed old language on reload
  → Added localStorage.setItem() before window.location.href navigation
- Contact form redirect (sendmail.php) hardcoded /pages/bestaetigung.html
  → Now uses language-aware redirect (/hu/bestaetigung etc.)
- /bestaetigung pretty URL was missing from .htaccess rewrite rules
  → Added RewriteRule for /bestaetigung → /pages/bestaetigung.html

New test suite: tests/e2e/lang-navigation.spec.ts (76 tests)
- CSS/JS asset loading on lang-prefixed pages
- Nav and footer link language prefix retention
- Language switcher URL navigation (all directions incl. back to German)
- /bestaetigung route for all 9 languages
- Apartment card links with correct language prefix

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ci: add FTP deploy workflows for dev and main branches

- deploy-dev.yml: push to dev → FTP /dev/ (staging)
- deploy-prod.yml: push to main → FTP /dist/ (production)
- Both support manual trigger (workflow_dispatch)
- Excludes: tests/, node_modules/, docker files, .env, playwright artifacts
- Uses sync-state for incremental deploys (no full wipe)
- Requires GitHub Secrets: FTP_HOST, FTP_USER, FTP_PASSWORD

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): use FTPS protocol for Strato FTP connection

* fix(ci): switch from FTP to rsync over SSH for Strato deployment

* Fix deployment path in deploy-dev.yml

* fix: use relative rsync target paths for dev and prod deployments

Changed :/dev/ → :dev/ and :/dist/ → :dist/ so rsync resolves paths
relative to the SSH home directory (webspace root) instead of the
Linux system /dev/ device directory.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(ci): generate config files from GitHub Secrets on deploy

Both deploy workflows now create api/config.local.php and
api/weekly_mail_config.php from GitHub Secrets before rsync,
eliminating the need to manually upload config files via FTP.

Also updates CHANGELOG with v1.5.1 and v1.5.2 entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: bump CHANGELOG to v2.0.0 — first production release

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
ci: fix deploy — vercel build on runner with env vars, prebuilt deploy

* feat(db): add Prisma schema with User, Profile, Document, NDA models

- User model with role (CREATIVE/COMPANY) and plan enum
- ProfileCreative: fullName, birthDate, birthPlace, codiceFiscale, partitaIva
- ProfileCompany: ragioneSociale, formaGiuridica, sede, SDI, referente
- Document: file metadata + SHA-256 hash for IP timestamping
- NdaAgreement + NdaClause + NdaAgreementClause junction table
- prisma.config.ts for datasource configuration

* chore: update dependencies and config for MVP stack

- Add tRPC, @tanstack/react-query, NextAuth, Prisma, bcryptjs, zod
- Add Shadcn/ui, lucide-react, class-variance-authority
- Update .gitignore for generated prisma client
- Add Shadcn components.json config

* feat(api): add tRPC routers, NextAuth config, and Prisma client

- tRPC context with session + prisma, public + protected procedures
- Auth router: 7 multi-step signup procedures + me query
- Document router: create, list, getById with SHA-256 hash
- NDA router: create, list, getById, accept (clickwrap with IP logging)
- NextAuth credentials provider with JWT strategy
- Prisma client singleton with pg adapter
- TypeScript augmentations for next-auth session types

* feat(api): add NextAuth and tRPC API route handlers

- /api/auth/[...nextauth] — NextAuth handler
- /api/trpc/[trpc] — tRPC fetch adapter handler

* feat(ui): add Shadcn/ui components and SessionProvider

- Accordion, Badge, Button, Card, Checkbox, Input, Label, Separator, Tabs
- NextAuth SessionProvider wrapper for client components

* feat(auth): implement multi-step signup and login flow

- Splash page with rainbow gradient logo + auto-redirect
- Login page: email/password + SPID/CIE layout buttons (disabled)
- Profile selection: Creativo (yellow) / Azienda (blue) cards
- Multi-step signup wizard with progress bar (6 steps creative, 7 company)
- StepSignup, StepPersonalInfo, StepCompanyInfo, StepCompanyReferent
- StepPhone, StepOTP (mock: accepts 123456), StepPlan

* feat(dashboard): add home feed, NDA creation, and document upload

- Home feed with FeedCard component (preview, title, type badge, SHA hash)
- FilterMenu with type filter (Timestamp/NDA/Contract) and sort
- Tabs (Tutti/Inviati/Non inviati) and FAB button
- NDA creation form: title, description, duration selector, clause checklist
- Document upload: drag & drop, client-side SHA-256 hash, title input

* feat(layout): dark mode root layout with TRPCProvider

- Dark mode HTML class + Inter/Geist fonts
- TRPCProvider + SessionProvider wrappers
- Shadcn/ui CSS design tokens (dark theme)
- Splash page with rainbow gradient logo

* chore(docker): add multi-stage Dockerfile and compose

- Multi-stage build: deps → build (prisma generate) → production runner
- docker-compose.yml: PostgreSQL 16 + app with health checks
- .dockerignore for optimized build context
- Enable standalone output in next.config.ts

* docs: update README and AGENTS, refresh .gitignore

* fix: code review — type safety, route protection, remove starter assets

- NdaStatus enum in schema; add recipientId index
- protectedProcedure narrows session to non-null
- Remove OTP fallback hardcode (throws BAD_REQUEST if no OTP)
- Remove unused name state in StepSignup
- FeedCard: replace manual DocItem with inferRouterOutputs
- nda/new: replace CLAUSE_TEMPLATES with trpc.nda.getClauses query
- BrandLogo component; remove inline gradient from login and home
- proxy.ts: route protection via withAuth (Next.js 16 convention)
- staleTime: 60_000 on QueryClient
- NEXTAUTH_SECRET guard at boot
- Remove default Next.js starter SVGs from public/

* feat(ui): brand redesign — splash sequence, profile select, login restyling

- Splash: motion SVG → black → logo fade-in/scale; tap or 3s auto-navigate
- BrandLogo: per-letter brand colors, withIcon prop (icon-logo.svg)
- Rename iconLogo.svg → icon-logo.svg (kebab-case convention)
- Button: rounded-full pill shape globally
- Login: SPID/CIE pill buttons with green border, privacy links highlighted, fade-out on success
- Profile select: vertical cards with brand copy, info modal (Dialog) per profile type
- Add shadcn Dialog component
- Add prisma initial migration

* fix: security hardening — rate limiting, OTP, password denylist, tRPC session fix

* fix: resolve Copilot review — tab filtering, NDA guard, key prop, rename step, remove rate limiter; add MVP philosophy to AGENTS

* Update src/app/page.tsx

commit.message:
Fix VS Code install on Trixie: use apt-get to resolve deps

dpkg -i does not handle missing dependencies. Replaced with
apt-get install on the local .deb so transitive deps are pulled
automatically — required after the Bookworm→Trixie migration.

commit.message:
feat!: inline plugin skills config, workspace schema v2 (#233) (#236)

BREAKING CHANGE: skill selection is now configured inline on each
plugin entry instead of top-level disabledSkills/enabledSkills arrays.

- Add PluginSkillsConfig type: string[] (allowlist) or {exclude:string[]} (blocklist)
- Add skills field to PluginEntrySchema object variant
- Add version field to WorkspaceConfigSchema
- Update getAllSkillsFromPlugins and collectPluginSkills to read inline skills
- Add pluginSkillsMode to SkillInfo for caller convenience
- Update workspace-modify and user-workspace write functions to write inline
- Simplify plugin-skills CLI: remove inAllowlistMode detection, use SkillInfo.pluginSkillsMode
- Add migrateWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 / migrateUserWorkspaceSkillsV1toV2 called on sync
- Bump to v1.0.0, add CHANGELOG.md

Migration: automatic on next workspace sync. No manual action required.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: allagents
repository.description: CLI tool for managing multi-repo AI agent workspaces with plugin synchronization across multiple AI clients.

commit.message:
feat: auto-wrap flat SKILL.md repos as skills (#232) (#234)

When a plugin has no skills/ subdirectory, scan the plugin root for
directories containing SKILL.md and treat each as a skill. This gives
compatibility with the npx skills ecosystem (e.g. vercel-labs/agent-skills)
without any schema changes.

The enabledSkills/disabledSkills schema continues to work unchanged
since the plugin is still tracked as a workspace plugin entry.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: support installing skills individually (#228) (#231)

- Fix plugin install --skill when plugin already installed: detect
  'Plugin already exists' error and if --skill flags provided, skip
  the install step and proceed directly to enabling skills + sync

- Add top-level `allagents skills` command as shorthand alias for
  `allagents plugin skills` (list/add/remove), giving UX parity
  with `npx skills`

- Enhance `skills add` with --from/-f option: if skill not found in
  installed plugins, installs the specified plugin source first then
  enables the skill (mirrors `npx skills add <pkg> --skill <name>`)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(timeout): add deadline and timeout utilities (#498)

New package for running functions with time limits:
- Do[T]: run function with timeout, return result or timeout error
- DoSimple: simplified Do for functions returning only error
- After[T]: run function async, receive result on channel
- Race[T]: run multiple functions, return first result

All functions are context-aware and cancel child contexts on completion.
Includes doc.go, 15+ unit tests, 2 benchmarks, 3 examples.

Co-authored-by: Jordan <jordan@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Scribe: OB-Xd VST3 bridge integration session complete

- Orchestration logs: Spike (architecture), Jet (deployment), Faye (host diagnostics), Ed (verification), Coordinator (integration)
- Session log: Final bridge message-pump refactor validated
- Decision inbox merged: 6 entries covering IPlugFrame fix, versioned deployment, thread affinity root cause
- Cross-agent history updated: Team learnings documented
- Archive check: decisions.md at 152KB, entries all recent (no archiving needed)

Architectural invariant locked:
> The bridge main thread is the UI/STA thread. All window creation, COM operations, plugin loads, and IPlugView calls happen on this thread. Pipe reader is background thread only.

Validation complete:
✅ Shipped Debug bridge byte-matches rebuilt Release bridge
✅ load_ack succeeds; openEditor successfully calls attached() without deadlock
✅ OB-Xd editor incomplete UI confirmed as plugin-side (beyond minimal host scope)
✅ Versioned deployment + manifest working end-to-end
⚠️ Test coverage gap flagged: 0 automated tests currently cover VST3/UI behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix VST3 editor deadlock: move pipe I/O off main thread

Root cause: JUCE-based plugins (OB-Xd) bind their MessageManager to the
thread that loads the plugin DLL (the bridge main thread). The editor
window was created on a separate editorThread_. When attached() called
MessageManager::callFunctionOnMessageThread(), JUCE posted to the main
thread and blocked -- but the main thread was stuck in ReadFile(pipe).

Fix: refactor Bridge::Run() so the main thread runs a Win32 message
loop (GetMessageW). Pipe reading moves to a background thread that
posts WM_BRIDGE_COMMAND to a hidden message-only window. All VST3
plugin loads and editor operations now happen on the main thread, which
is both the COM/STA thread and the JUCE MessageManager thread.

Key changes:
- bridge.h/cpp: main thread runs message loop; PipeReaderLoop on
  background thread; DrainCommandQueue dispatches via window message
- audio_renderer.h/cpp: removed editorThread_, promise/future, timeout;
  OpenEditor is synchronous on calling thread; CloseEditor is direct
  cleanup with no thread join
- EditorHostWindowProc: WM_CLOSE calls CloseEditor for proper VST3
  teardown; uses SetPropW/GetPropW instead of GWLP_USERDATA
- CMakeLists.txt: added commoniids.cpp for IPlugFrame/IPlugView IIDs
- bridge shutdown: ipc_.Close() before join to unblock ReadFile
- OleInitialize moved to Bridge::Run() main thread (was editor thread)

Also includes prior-session uncommitted fixes:
- Version-stamped bridge deployment (csproj)
- IComponentHandler stub in HostApplication
- ParseEditorAck ok-field check + ResolveBridgeExecutablePath

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: correct SqlMetaData constructor selection for datetime2/datetimeoffset/time TVP columns

The previous fix for #393 used `match name, dbType with` where `name`
is the column name (e.g. "CreatedAt"), not the SQL type name. This meant
the pattern | "datetime2", SqlDbType.DateTime2 -> ... would never fire
unless the column happened to be literally named "datetime2".

The correct fix is to match on `dbType` alone, which always contains the
actual SQL type. This ensures that all DateTime2, DateTimeOffset and Time
TVP columns use SqlMetaData(name, dbType, 0uy, scale) rather than
SqlMetaData(name, dbType, precision, scale), avoiding the ArgumentException
"Metadata for field 'X' did not match the original record's metadata"
when the precision byte doesn't equal what SQL Server expects.

Closes #393

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: FSharp.Data.SqlClient
repository.description: A set of F# Type Providers for statically typed access to MS SQL database

commit.message:
feat: add `export-audit` CLI command for wallet transaction CSV export (#7700)

Wallet needs to export transactions as a CSV for auditing, with optional
date range filtering and fiat conversion.

## Changes

### `cli.rs`
- New `ExportAuditArgs` struct with: `--output-file`, `--start-date`,
`--end-date`, `--conversion-rate`, `--currency` (default: `USD`)
- New `ExportAudit(ExportAuditArgs)` variant in `CliCommands`
- `parse_utc_datetime` helper accepting RFC3339 and `YYYY-MM-DD` /
`YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS` formats

### `commands.rs`
- `ExportAudit` handler: fetches all completed transactions, filters by
date range, calls `write_audit_to_csv_file`
- `write_audit_to_csv_file`: emits CSV with columns matching the spec —
transaction type is `Deposit` (inbound/coinbase) or `Withdraw`
(outbound); transaction hash derived from first kernel hash; amounts in
XTM (Minotari); optional fiat column using supplied rate

### `wallet_modes.rs`
- Added `ExportAudit` arm to the exhaustive command-match test

## Example

```bash
minotari_console_wallet export-audit \
  --output-file audit.csv \
  --start-date 2021-01-01 \
  --end-date 2021-12-31 \
  --conversion-rate 3130.0 \
  --currency USD
```

Output CSV columns:
```
ID, Transaction Hash, Status, Transaction Type, DateTime (UTC),
From Address, To Address, Amount, AmountTicker, Amount (USD),
Txn Fee, FeeTicker, Fee (USD)
```

fix: resolve 7 failing Android unit tests

- DriftDetector: remove off-by-one (+1) in consistentReadings count
- DriftDetectorTest: fix manyPoints test to produce ascending timestamps
  with ascending distances (was reversed, causing decreasing distances)
- CatenaryCalculatorTest: widen integration test bound from 8x to 9x depth
  to account for 20% safety margin on 7:1 scope ratio (~8.3x)
- GeoCalculationsTest: use 99.9m instead of exact 100.0m for boundary test
  to avoid float precision mismatch between destinationPoint and distanceMeters
- ProtocolMessageParser: add testOptions.unitTests.isReturnDefaultValues=true
  to build.gradle.kts so android.util.Log calls don't throw in unit tests

commit.message:
docs(schemas): write task format reference documentation (T123)

Create docs/TASK_FORMAT.md with comprehensive reference for both
import formats (markdown and JSON). Covers metadata table fields,
headed sections, backlog.json and flat array schemas, field mapping
tables, type/priority mapping, complete examples, an AI prompt
template for format conversion, and troubleshooting guide.

All field mappings verified against actual parser implementations
and Zod schemas.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: address PR #47 review comments

- Fix shallow copy bug: clone answered object before mutation in handleAnswer
- Add CRLF to END:VCALENDAR for RFC 5545 compliance
- Wrap stopMonitoring DB/wear ops in try/finally to guarantee service stop
- Add comments explaining intentional useEffect dependency omissions
- Add CSS fallback comment for safe-area padding

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(web-ui): add Batch Create Tasks dialog to Tasks page

Add BatchCreateDialog component that lets operators paste a JSON array
of task objects to create multiple tasks at once. Includes client-side
validation with per-item error reporting, preview summary, and wires
to the existing useCreateTaskBatch hook.

- BatchCreateDialog.tsx: monospace textarea, validate/create flow
- BatchCreateDialog.test.tsx: 29 tests (unit + component)
- TasksPage: "Create Batch" button between Import and Create
- T130 marked done, E025 now complete (8/8)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Filter ResizeObserver loop errors from dev server overlay (#30125)

The @rspack/dev-server overlay catches ResizeObserver loop errors as
runtime crashes, showing a full-screen error overlay that blocks
development. These errors are benign — they originate from
lit-virtualizer (used by ha-data-table) and simply mean not all
resize observations could be delivered in a single animation frame.

Add a client.overlay.runtimeErrors filter to the dev server config
that suppresses ResizeObserver loop messages while still surfacing
all real runtime errors.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix gallery integration card crash from invalid mock hassUrl (#30126)

The gallery mock set hass.auth.data.hassUrl to an empty string, which
caused brandsUrl() to throw when constructing a URL via new URL(base, '').
In production hassUrl is always a valid URL, so the mock now uses
location.origin to match real behavior.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
